design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed TV-world levels with varied traversal methods (running, jumping, climbing, swinging, throwing), defeat enemies and bosses to progress, collect items and secrets
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Environmental hazards and enemy encounters against forward momentum; later levels shift to maze-like exploration requiring route discovery
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Garfield trapped inside a television world, fighting the Glitch monster that caused his predicament while discovering secrets and bonus challenges
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed platformer that emphasizes large, maze-like level design with branching routes in later stages rather than pure linear progression; integrates licensed IP's comedic tone through commercial break sequences and varied weapon/attack systems that change per level

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives; respawn at nearest checkpoint upon defeat; game over when all lives lost
difficulty.continues_system: Continue option allows resuming from beginning of last level reached; password/save system also available
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoint-based system where players respawn at nearest checkpoint within levels
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can discover alternate routes in maze-like later levels; bonus rounds offer extra lives and continues; items and weapon ammunition collection impacts combat readiness

progression.upgrade_system: Level-specific weapon/object systems (close-range weapons and thrown objects change between levels); beneficial items and ammunition scattered throughout for collection
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss defeats unlock next level progression; commercial break sequences reward extra lives or continues
replayability_hooks: Multiple routes in maze-like later levels encouraging exploration

world_building_techniques: Commercial break sequences between boss defeats
Licensed IP integration (Jim Davis' Garfield comic strip characters and tone)
Electronic monster (Glitch) as central narrative threat

boss_design.philosophy: Bosses serve as level gates requiring defeat to progress; encountered at end of each themed level after traversing obstacles and enemies

danger_signals: Varied enemy types (ghosts, piranhas, crabs, bulldogs, mummified mice)
environmental_storytelling: TV-world setting with themed levels (visual narrative of being trapped inside television)
Electronic/glitchy aesthetic of Glitch monster reflects origin from spare TV parts
Level design progression: early linear, later maze-like (mirrors increasing complexity of escape challenge)
